descriptions: The Errol Flynns are the precursors to the Fenkell Mafia Killers, meaning the Fenkell Mafia Killers succeeded them.
edges.evidence_quote: The Errol Flynns are recognized as the precursors to most, if not all, Detroit gangs that followed in their wake. Some of the most notable successors include the "Be Like Boys", "Dexter Boys" (an offshoot of YBI), "Schoolcraft Boys or SCB's", "SNS", "Fenkell Boys", "FMK" ("Fenkell Mafia Killers"), "7 Mile Killers or 7 Mile Dogs", "Linwood Boys", "Brewster Boys", "Jeffries Boys", and "8 Mile sconys", ("JBFL") "Joy Boys" or "Exit 9"

Florencia 13.description: American criminal street gang based in Los Angeles, California, composed mainly of Mexican Americans. Named after the Florence area of Los Angeles County and controlled by the Mexican Mafia. Involved in drug trafficking, murder, assault, robbery, and extortion. Active since the 1930s with an estimated 2,000-3,000+ members.

Irish mob.description: The Irish mob is a collection of organized crime groups composed of largely ethnic Irish members operating primarily in the United States since the early 19th century. Originating in Irish-American street gangs, it has appeared in most major U.S. and Canadian cities, especially in the Northeast and Midwest. Notable historical gangs include the Dead Rabbits, Whyos, and the Westies. The group has been involved in various criminal activities such as murder, drug trafficking, and racketeering.

descriptions: The American Mafia historically rivals the Irish mob.

Ichiwa-kai.description: The Ichiwa-kai was a yakuza gang based in Osaka, Japan, formed on June 13, 1984 by Hiroshi Yamamoto after a split from the Yamaguchi-gumi. It had over 10,000 members and engaged in a violent war with the Yamaguchi-gumi following the assassination of its boss. The organization dissolved in March 1989.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai and Yamaguchi-gumi fought a war after Ichiwa-kai assassinated the Yamaguchi-gumi boss.
edges.evidence_quote: The killing of the Yamaguchi Godfather sparked a Kansai -wide yakuza war between the two groups in which over 20 gangsters were killed.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi and Ichiwa-kai were in a violent gang war.
edges.evidence_quote: The war between Yamaguchi Gumi and the splinter group killed more than 30 gang members and a few bystanders.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai was formed by a splinter from Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: It was formed on June 13, 1984 when Hiroshi Yamamoto , a top lieutenant in the Yamaguchi-gumi , broke from that gang to form his own organization with over 10,000 members.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai is a splinter group from Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Masahisa Takenaka was gunned down in 1985 by the Ichiwa-kai gang, a Yamaguchi Gumi splinter gang.

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai seceded from Yamaguchi-gumi and assassinated its boss, sparking the Yama–Ichi War.
edges.evidence_quote: However, Takenaka was assassinated at a girlfriend's home in Osaka early the following year by a rival group, the Ichiwa-kai , led by Hiroshi Yamamoto, who had seceded from the clan.

descriptions: Yamaguchi-gumi fought against Ichiwa-kai in the Yama-Ichi War.
edges.evidence_quote: He has been involved in the Yamaguchi-gumi's important yakuza wars such as ... Yama-Ichi War (1984–1989, against the Ichiwa-kai).

descriptions: Ichiwa-kai broke away and engaged in war with Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.evidence_quote: Hiroshi Yamamoto ... broke away from Yamaguchi-gumi ... to form the Ichiwa-kai. A bitter rivalry existed between the two groups, which led to an all-out war (the Yama-Ichi War)

Black Disciples.description: African American street gang based in Chicago, Illinois, founded in 1966 by David Barksdale. Originally formed as the Devil's Disciples, it later merged with the Gangster Disciples to form the Black Gangster Disciples Nation before splitting again. The gang is involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, murder, and racketeering, with an estimated 15,000 members.

Supreme Eiye Confraternity.description: The Supreme Eiye Confraternity (SEC) is a Nigerian confraternity founded in 1965 at the University of Ibadan. Originally a student group, it has evolved into a criminal organization involved in drug trafficking, passport forgery, money laundering, and sex trafficking. It operates internationally with cells called 'forums' and is outlawed under Nigeria's Secret Cult and Similar Activities Prohibition Act.

descriptions: Black Axe and Eiye confraternity engaged in violent fights.
edges.evidence_quote: At least 53 people were injured or killed between March 2009 and June 2013 in fights between the Black Axe and their rivals, the Eiye confraternity.

descriptions: The Supreme Eiye Confraternity has a long-standing rivalry with Black Axe, involving killings of rival members.
edges.evidence_quote: The confraternity has a long-standing rivalry with Black Axe, another Nigerian confraternity-affiliated group.

Gang des postiches.description: The Gang des postiches was a team of bank robbers that operated in Paris between 1981 and 1986, robbing around thirty banks. They used disguises including false mustaches, beards, and wigs. The gang was centered around individuals from Belleville who had been friends since childhood. They were known for being respectful to hostages and were mythified by the press.

descriptions: The methods of the Gang des Tractions Avant were continued by the Gang des Postiches.
edges.evidence_quote: were mostly continued by a number of other gangs, notably the gang des postiches

'Ndrina Mazzagatti.description: The Mazzagatti are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Oppido Mamertina, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Polimeni and Bonarrigo families. Active since at least the 1970s, they have been involved in cement business, extortion, and a long-running feud with the Ferraro-Raccosta clan. In 2007, authorities seized assets worth €120 million from alleged members.

descriptions: The infobox lists NAR as an ally of Banda della Magliana.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana laundered money from NAR robberies and NAR members collected debts for the Banda.
edges.evidence_quote: Carminati iniziò ad affidare i proventi delle rapine di autofinanziamento effettuate con i NAR, in modo da poterli riciclare in altre attività illecite quali l'usura o lo spaccio di droga; viceversa recuperava per Giuseppucci soldi che il boss prestava a strozzo intascandosi una 'stecca' del 10-15%.

descriptions: Banda della Magliana commissioned NAR members to commit murders on their behalf.
edges.evidence_quote: la Banda, di tanto in tanto commissionava ai giovani fascisti anche di eliminare alcune persone poco gradite, come nel caso del tabaccaio romano Teodoro Pugliese, ucciso nell'aprile del 1980 da Carminati

descriptions: The Banda della Magliana and the NAR had converging interests and collaborated in the Bologna massacre cover-up.
edges.evidence_quote: La probabile convergenza d'interessi tra gli uomini della Magliana, gli ambienti dell'eversione nera e alcuni settori deviati dei servizi e della politica, trova perfetta esemplificazione nel tentativo di depistaggio legato alla strage alla stazione ferroviaria di Bologna del 2 agosto 1980, per la quale vennero riconosciuti esecutori materiali (tra gli altri) alcuni militanti dei Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari, di Valerio Fioravanti.

descriptions: Andrea Buonpadre acted as a liaison between Banda della Magliana and far-right terrorism, including NAR.
edges.evidence_quote: fa da tramite tra la banda, Cosa Nostra e il Terrorismo nero

descriptions: Massimo Carminati, a founder of Mafia Capitale, was a former member of Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari.
edges.evidence_quote: Massimo Carminati, a former member of Nuclei Armati Rivoluzionari and Banda della Magliana

descriptions: Mafia Capitale has its roots in the robberies of NAR.
edges.evidence_quote: questa organizzazione operava a Roma già a partire dal 2000 circa, affondando le sue radici nelle rapine dei NAR degli anni ottanta e successivamente nella stessa banda della Magliana.

King Cobras.description: The King Cobras are a prominent Pacific gang in New Zealand, founded by Samoans in Ponsonby, Auckland in the 1950s. They are the oldest gang in New Zealand, with an estimated 400 members. The gang is involved in drug dealing, assault, and murder, and has a history of violence and firearm possession.

descriptions: King Cobras are listed as allies of Black Power in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Crips , Killa Beez (gang) , King Cobras (gang) New Zealand Nomads

edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, members of the King Cobras ran a large methamphetamine ring along with the Head Hunters in one of Aucklands biggest drug cases.

Greenwich Village Crew.description: The Greenwich Village Crew is a crew within the Genovese crime family, active in the Greenwich Village area of Manhattan. Originally controlled by Vito Genovese from the 1920s, it was later led by Vincent Gigante. The crew engages in racketeering, gambling, loan sharking, and extortion. It remains active, though its current leadership is uncertain.

descriptions: The Greenwich Village Crew is allied with the Bonanno crime family.

edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Bonanno , Colombo , Gambino and Lucchese crime families

Siderno Group.description: The Siderno Group is a criminal association linked to the 'Ndrangheta, operating in Canada, Australia, Italy, and the United States. It originated from the town of Siderno in Calabria, with members migrating to Canada and Australia in the 1950s. The group is involved in global cocaine trafficking and money laundering, with connections across South America and Europe. In Canada, it has been active since the 1950s, particularly in the Greater Toronto Area, and maintains close ties to Calabrian clans.

descriptions: The Siderno Group is a part of the 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Commisso was related to Cosimo 'The Quail' Commisso of Siderno, Italy, considered by police there to be an 'Ndrangheta organized crime boss' of the Siderno Group.

descriptions: 'Ndrangheta established the Siderno Group for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Già negli anni ottanta furono in grado di mettere in piedi un traffico di droga in tre continenti, il cosiddetto Siderno Group : dal Canada all' Australia , dal Sud America all' Italia .

descriptions: Siderno Group is a consortium of families from Siderno, which is part of 'Ndrangheta's structure.
edges.evidence_quote: Negli anni cinquanta proprio in Canada e Australia nasce il cosiddetto Siderno Group , un consorzio di famiglie originarie di Siderno con collegamenti anche con elementi di Cosa nostra statunitense come Frank Costello dediti al traffico di eroina e di armi.

edges.evidence_quote: The 'ndrina is based in Siderno, but also has a branch in the Greater Toronto Area in Canada as part of the Siderno Group.

edges.evidence_quote: Sono anche tra coloro che le forze dell'ordine canadesi in sinergia con le forze dell'ordine italiane hanno ribattezzato Siderno Group.

Fresno Bulldogs.description: Primarily Mexican American criminal street and prison gang based in Fresno, California, founded in 1984. One of the biggest drug gangs in Central California with an estimated 12,000 members. Engaged in drug trafficking, human trafficking, murder, and other crimes. Unaffiliated with Sureños or Norteños, and rivals with multiple prison gangs.

Solid Wood Soldiers.description: Neo-Nazi prison gang and organized crime syndicate based in Texas, founded in 1990 at Snake River Correctional Institution in Oregon. Involved in drug trafficking, murder, extortion, and assault. Known for tattoos featuring SWS initials with lightning bolts and symbols like a bear claw with the number 4. Active in Texas with ties to other white supremacist groups.

Puccio family.description: The Puccio family was an Argentine criminal family active in the early 1980s, based in San Isidro, Buenos Aires. The family, led by father Arquímedes Puccio, was convicted of four kidnappings and three murders. They targeted wealthy individuals for ransom, killing most victims despite receiving payment. The family was arrested in 1985 after a police raid freed their last victim.

March 23 Movement.description: A Congolese Rwandan-backed rebel paramilitary group based in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, operating mainly in North Kivu and South Kivu. Established in 2012 by former members of the National Congress for the Defence of the People (CNDP) who mutinied against the government. It is the principal member of the Congo River Alliance and has been involved in multiple rebellions, including the capture of Goma in 2012. The group remains active as of 2025.

edges.target: Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain
descriptions: APCLS signed an agreement to halt hostilities among themselves, which includes M23 as a party to the ceasefire.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 May, various armed factions—including the Alliance des Patriotes pour un Congo Libre et Souverain (APCLS)... signed an agreement in Pinga, Walikale Territory, pledging to halt hostilities among themselves and align with the Congolese army.

edges.target: Alliance Nationale des Congolais pour la Défense des Droits de l'Homme
descriptions: ANCDH signed an agreement to halt hostilities among themselves, which includes M23 as a party to the ceasefire.
edges.evidence_quote: On 9 May, various armed factions—including ... the Alliance Nationale des Congolais pour la Défense des Droits de l'Homme (ANCDH)—signed an agreement in Pinga, Walikale Territory, pledging to halt hostilities among themselves and align with the Congolese army.

edges.target: Armed Forces of the Democratic Republic of the Congo

Armenian mafia.description: The Armenian mafia is a general term for organized criminal groups composed of ethnic Armenians, structured in clans called yekhpayrutyuns. Based in Armenia, it operates internationally in countries including Germany, France, Spain, Russia, and the United States. The groups are involved in drug trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and other crimes. They have been the subject of investigations by German, French, and Spanish authorities.

descriptions: Albanian mafia colludes with Armenian mafia in Germany.
edges.evidence_quote: The Albanian mafia is involved in smuggling heroin and cocaine into Germany in collusion with Italian, Armenian and Chechen mafia groups.

descriptions: The American Mafia is allied with the Armenian mafia.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... African-American mob , Armenian mafia , Chaldean mafia , Corsican mafia , Dixie Mafia , Greek mafia , Jewish-American mob , Lebanese mafia , Mexican Mafia , Russian mafia , Serbian mafia , various motorcycle gangs (such as the Hells Angels , Outlaws , and Pagan's ) , various street gangs (such as the Bloods , Crips , and Latin Kings )

descriptions: Infobox lists Calabrian mafia as an ally of Armenian mafia.

descriptions: Armenian gangsters and Georgian mafiosi were part of the same criminal syndicate in France and Belgium in 2018.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 2018, the French police conducted an operation codenamed VORS 69-01 against a criminal syndicate operating in France and Belgium , which included Azerbaijani mafiosi , Georgian mafiosi and Armenian gangsters.
